prompt: Implement three independent reliability units from the same base commit. Unit A: in core/browser_manager.py make download detection deterministic and testable through narrow dependency injection, ignore incomplete downloads, choose a stable new MP3 candidate, use bounded polling, and preserve existing public call behavior; add mock-only tests/test_browser_manager.py. Unit B: in services/text_processor.py make the voice catalog immutable/data-driven with O(1) value lookup, remove the duplicate visible voice label, keep the existing fallback, strengthen empty-input language validation, and make combined LRC generation deterministic when text/audio lengths differ; add mock-only tests/test_text_processor.py. Unit C: in core/conversion_engine.py support constructor dependency injection without changing zero-argument behavior, initialize driver safely before try/finally, replace unbounded audio-file readiness waiting with a configurable bounded wait, preserve source-to-audio alignment when lines fail or are skipped, and return a small structured conversion summary while retaining callbacks; add mock-only tests/test_conversion_engine.py. The three units must remain independently reviewable and must not modify each other's files.
acceptance: Each unit has deterministic mock-only tests and performs no network, browser, audio, or GUI operation.
The three source/test path pairs are non-overlapping and can be implemented concurrently from the same base commit.
Existing zero-argument constructors and existing public method call forms remain compatible.
No broad formatting, generated files, bytecode, or unrelated edits are present.
All three narrow test commands, py_compile, and git diff --check pass.
